updates.title: Claude Design is the best feature Anthropic's launched in a while, but it has some serious limitations
updates.summary: Claude Design is Anthropic Labs' AI-powered design tool currently in Research Preview, allowing users to create presentations, social media graphics, UI mockups, website wireframes, and infographics through natural language prompts — positioning it as a direct alternative to Figma and Canva. XDA's hands-on review praises it as Anthropic's best recent product launch, noting how the natural-language creation flow dramatically lowers the barrier for non-designers. However, the piece highlights serious limitations: access is restricted to paid Claude subscriptions, and a weekly usage quota depletes faster than any other Claude limit, making the tool feel artificially constrained.
updates.accuracy_notes: Verified — Claude Design launched April 17, 2026 under Anthropic Labs and is confirmed in Research Preview at design.claude.ai. Usage limit complaints are consistent across multiple independent user reports. The comparison to Figma/Canva is editorially sound. No misleading claims detected.

updates.title: I added Claude Code's memory to my workflows, and my automation became effortless
updates.summary: Anthropic has built persistent memory into Claude Code through two complementary systems: Auto Memory (which automatically captures and saves project context, code style preferences, debugging insights, and architecture notes across sessions) and CLAUDE.md files (plain-text markdown files containing persistent instructions loaded at every session start). The article demonstrates a practical integration — having automation pipelines invoke the Claude Code CLI inside a project directory, whereupon it loads all saved memory, settings, hooks, and repository structure automatically. The result is significantly more personalised and efficient automated workflows that no longer require context re-establishment each run.
updates.accuracy_notes: Verified — Auto Memory and CLAUDE.md are confirmed production Claude Code features, available since v2.1.59+. The automation integration approach described is technically accurate and consistent with official documentation at code.claude.com/docs/en/memory. No red flags or exaggerated claims.
